title: pseudopilocereus superbus
side_effects: Pseudopilocereus superbus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea. Prolonged use of the plant may also lead to allergic reactions, resulting in symptoms like hives, itching, and swelling. Additionally, improper use of pseudopilocereus superbus can cause interactions with other medications, potentially leading to adverse effects. In severe cases, improper use may even result in respiratory problems, such as shortness of breath or difficulty breathing.
